heading: Refusal of clearance and entry
label: 42107 Refusal of clearance and entry
notes: Historical and Revision NotesRevisedSectionSource (U.S. Code)Source (Statutes at Large)4210746 App.:876(j).; as added , , ; , , .June 5, 1920, ch. 250, § 19(j)Pub. L. 101–595, title I, § 103(2)104 Stat. 2979Pub. L. 105–258, title III, § 301112 Stat. 1915Nov. 16, 1990Oct. 14, 1998In paragraph (1), the words “Secretary of Homeland Security” are substituted for “collector of customs at the port or place of destination in the United States” because the functions of the Customs Service and of the Secretary of the Treasury relating thereto were transferred to the Secretary of Homeland Security by section 403(1) of the Homeland Security Act of 2002 (, ). The functions of the collector of customs previously were vested in the Secretary of the Treasury by Reorganization Plan No. 26 of 1950, and the office of collector of customs previously was abolished by Reorganization Plan No. 1 of 1965.Pub. children.text: refuse the clearance required by to a vessel of a country that is named in a regulation prescribed by the Commission under ; andsection 60105 of this titlesection 42101 of this title

children.label: A
children.text: deny entry, for purposes of oceanborne trade, of a vessel of a country that is named in a regulation prescribed by the Commission under , to a port or place in the United States or the navigable waters of the United States; orsection 42101 of this title
